questionHtml:
Sunil lent Rs. 5,000 to Sudhir for 2 years and Rs. 3,000 to Vinod for 4 years at the same simple interest rate. In total, he received Rs. 4,400 as interest from both borrowers. What is the rate of interest per annum?

  1. 17%
  2. 12%
  3. 15%
  4. 20%
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A person invested Rs. 10,000 in two schemes - Scheme A at 12% p.a. and Scheme B at 8% p.a., both under simple interest. After 1 year, the total interest from both schemes was Rs. 1,000. How much was invested in Scheme A?

  1. Rs. 2,000
  2. Rs. 3,000
  3. Rs. 5,000
  4. Rs. 2,800
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
Mohan borrowed a sum of money at simple interest of 7% per annum for the first 3 years, 5% per annum for the next 5 years, and 11% per annum for the period beyond 8 years. If he pays a total of Rs. 6800 as interest only at the end of 10 years, how much money did he borrow?

  1. Rs. 8000
  2. Rs. 9000
  3. Rs. 10000
  4. Rs. 12000
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
Aman needed Rs. 60,000 and split the sum between two lenders: Lender A at 18% p.a. and Lender B at 12% p.a. (simple interest). After 2 years, the total interest paid was Rs. 19,200. If he had interchanged the principal amounts, his interest would have been Rs. 2,400 less. How much did Aman borrow from Lender A at 18% p.a. ?

  1. Rs. 20,000
  2. Rs. 16,000
  3. Rs. 14,000
  4. Rs. 40,000
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
An individual invested certain amounts in three different schemes A, B, and C at simple interest rates of 7% p.a., 9% p.a., and 11% p.a. respectively. If the total interest accrued in one year was Rs. 2520 and the amount invested in Scheme C was 250% of the amount invested in Scheme A and 300% of the amount invested in Scheme B, what was the amount invested in Scheme A ?

  1. Rs. 3000
  2. Rs. 5500
  3. Rs. 6000
  4. Rs. 6500
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A sum of Rs. 15000 is lent out in two parts, one at 14% simple interest and the other at 20% simple interest. If the annual interest is Rs. 2400, the sum lent at 20% is:

  1. Rs. 5500
  2. Rs. 4400
  3. Rs. 4000
  4. Rs. 5000
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A sum of Rs. 9000 is lent out in two parts, one at 6% simple interest and the other at 9% simple interest. If the annual interest is Rs. 630, the sum lent at 6% is:

  1. Rs. 6000
  2. Rs. 6500
  3. Rs. 3000
  4. Rs. 3500
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
Ramesh has Rs. 15,000. He lends a part of it to Asha for 3 years at 10% simple interest and the remaining to Meena for 3 years at 12% simple interest. If the total interest received from both Asha and Meena after 3 years is Rs. 4,950, find the amount lent to Asha.

  1. Rs. 9,000
  2. Rs. 7,500
  3. Rs. 8,000
  4. Rs. 6,500
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
Rs. 1800 is lent at a certain rate of simple interest. After 9 months, another Rs. 1200 is lent at a rate that is 1.5 times the original rate. If the total simple interest after 1 year is Rs. 144, find the original rate.

  1. 6.4%
  2. 6.5%
  3. 4.2%
  4. 4.6%
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
Reena borrowed Rs. 1156 at simple interest for a number of years equal to the rate of interest. At the end of the loan term, she paid Rs. 289 in interest. What was the interest rate ?

  1. 7%
  2. 5%
  3. 18%
  4. 10%
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
Amar borrowed Rs. 6000 from Shankar on March 15, 2025, at 12% per annum simple interest. Amar decided to repay the loan on September 15, 2025. What is the total amount Amar must repay Shankar ?

  1. Rs. 6200.90
  2. Rs. 6362.96
  3. Rs. 6162.66
  4. Rs. 6862.56
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
Gaurav invested a total of Rs. 4400 in three different simple interest schemes at 4%, 5%, and 10% per annum for one year. At the end of one year, he received the same interest from all three schemes. How much money (in Rs. ) did he invest in the scheme with a 5 % interest rate?

  1. Rs. 1000
  2. Rs. 1600
  3. Rs. 2000
  4. Rs. 800
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
Suppose Rs. is invested, and after 𝑃 2 years at 5% simple interest, it yields Rs. 𝑄 interest. If Rs. is then invested for 2 years 𝑄 at 5% simple interest, it yields Rs. 𝑅 interest. Which of the following is true?

  1. Q = P + R
  2. Q² = PR
  3. P = Q + R
  4. P² = Q + R
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A sum becomes 3.1 times of itself in 7 years under simple interest. In how many years will it become 6 times itself at the same rate ?

  1. years
  2. years 27 3 50 3
  3. years
  4. years 31 3 19 3
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
Neha lent Rs. 14000 to John on 10 July 2024 at a simple interest rate of 8% per annum. John plans to pay back the entire amount on 10 October 2024. What is the total amount John needs to return?

  1. Rs. 14280
  2. Rs. 14209
  3. Rs. 14208
  4. Rs. 14820
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A person invested a total sum of Rs. 2700 in three different schemes of simple interest at 3%, 4%, and 6% per annum. At the end of one year, he got the same interest from all three schemes. What was the amount (in Rs. ) invested at 6% ?

  1. Rs. 1100
  2. Rs. 760
  3. Rs. 600
  4. Rs. 850
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
At what rate of simple interest will Rs. 3,000 become Rs. 4,020 in 5 years?

  1. 8.5%
  2. 5.5%
  3. 8.8%
  4. 6.8%
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
If P, Q, and R are three amounts of money such that Q is the simple interest on P, and R is the simple interest on Q, all for the same time and at the same rate of interest, then which of the following is true?

  1. P² = QR
  2. Q² = PR
  3. R² = PQ
  4. P + Q = R
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A shopkeeper took a loan of Rs. 7000 from a bank on 1 April 2023 with simple interest at 8% per annum. He decided to clear the loan on 1 October 2023. How much amount did he pay back to the bank?

  1. Rs. 7280.76
  2. Rs. 7581.25
  3. Rs. 7784.79
  4. Rs. 7790.00
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A certain sum becomes 6 times itself in 7 years at simple interest. In how many years will it become 14 times itself at the same rate ?

  1. 18 years
  2. 20 years 1 5 2 5
  3. 22 years
  4. 16 years 3 6 3 5
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
If the simple interest on a certain sum of money for 3 years at 8% per annum is Rs. 480, what would be the simple interest on the same sum for 5 years at 12% per annum ?

  1. Rs. 960
  2. Rs. 1,200
  3. Rs. 1,000
  4. Rs. 1,440
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A sum amounts to Rs. 5,800 in 2 years and Rs. 6,200 in 4 years at simple interest. What is the rate of interest per annum?

  1. 2%
  2. 4.1%
  3. 3.7%
  4. 1.5%
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A sum of Rs. 10000 is lent out in two parts, one at 4% simple interest and the other at 6% simple interest. If the annual interest is Rs. 520, the sum lent at 4% is:

  1. Rs. 7200
  2. Rs. 5500
  3. Rs. 6000
  4. Rs. 4000
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
If the simple interest on a certain sum of money for 2 years at 5% per annum is Rs. 500, what would be the simple interest on the same sum for 5 years at 6% per annum ?

  1. Rs. 950
  2. Rs. 1,500
  3. Rs. 1,150
  4. Rs. 1,200
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
Rs.3000 is lent out at 3% per annum simple interest for 3 years. Find the amount after 3 years.

  1. 2900
  2. 4000
  3. 3600
  4. 3270
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
If P = Rs. 2,500, R = 11.5%, and T = 2.5 years, find the simple interest.

  1. Rs. 775
  2. Rs. 715
  3. Rs. 718.75
  4. Rs. 700.75
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
In how many years will a sum of Rs. 8,000 give a simple interest of Rs. 3,000 at 12.5% per annum ?

  1. 3 years 3 months
  2. 2 years
  3. 3 years
  4. 2 years 6 months
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
Consider the following statements regarding simple interest: 1. The simple interest on Rs. 10,000 for 3 years at 15% per annum is Rs. 4,500. 2. If a sum of money doubles in 5 years at simple interest, then it will become 1.5 times in 3 years. 3. The simple interest rate required for an amount to become 4.5 times in 7 years is 50%. Of these statements, which are correct?

  1. Only 1 is correct
  2. 1 and 2 are correct
  3. 1 and 3 are correct
  4. All 1, 2 and 3 are correct
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
If P = Rs. 2,400, R = 37.5%, and T = 2 years, find the simple interest.

  1. Rs. 1000
  2. Rs. 1200
  3. Rs. 1800
  4. Rs. 2000
subject: Mathematics
